channel.title: 2026-03-18 AI × Science 文献日报 RSS
channel.description: 2026-03-18 当天筛选出的 AI × 物理 / 化学 / 材料交叉文献 RSS。

item.title: Nonstabilizerness estimation using graph neural networks
item.description:
📚 Machine Learning: Science and Technology
👤 Vincenzo Lipardi, Domenica Dibenedetto, Georgios Stamoulis, Evert van Nieuwenburg and Mark H M Winands
This article proposes a graph neural network (GNN) approach to estimate nonstabilizerness in quantum circuits, measured by the stabilizer Rényi entropy (SRE). Nonstabilizerness is a fundamental resource for quantum advantage, and efficient SRE estimations are highly beneficial in practical applications. We address the nonstabilizerness estimation problem through three supervised learning formulations starting from easier classification tasks to the more challenging regression task. Experimental results show that the proposed GNN manages to capture meaningful features from the graph-based circuit representation, resulting in robust generalization performances achieved across diverse scenarios. In classification tasks, the GNN is trained on product states and generalizes on circuits evolved…

item.title: [ASAP] Comprehensive Structural Descriptors Enable Machine Learning Prediction of Perovskite Band Characteristics
item.description:
📚 ACS
👤 Zixuan Ni, Zhirui Zhang, Yiming Chen, Daye Zheng, and Jian Xu
The Journal of Physical Chemistry Letters DOI: 10.1021/acs.jpclett.6c00030

item.title: Antiferromagnetism and Kekulé valence bond order in the honeycomb optical Su-Schrieffer-Heeger-Hubbard model
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Sohan Malkaruge Costa, Benjamin Cohen-Stead, and Steven Johnston
Author(s): Sohan Malkaruge Costa, Benjamin Cohen-Stead, and Steven Johnston The precise role of electron-phonon (e−ph) coupling in graphene and related materials on a honeycomb lattice is not yet fully understood, despite extensive research on these systems. Here, we perform sign-problem-free determinant quantum Monte Carlo simulations of the optical Su-Sc… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 115138] Published Wed Mar 18, 2026

item.title: CaFe₂O(PO₄)₂: A compound with Sphantomůle0.16em0ex=phantomůle0.16em0exfrac52 corner sharing triangular sawtooth chains
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 R. C. Moharana, Sk. Soyeb Ali, V. K. Sahu, J. Link, I. Heinmaa, S. Chattopadhyay, S. K. Panda, R. Stern, T. Chakrabarty, and B. Koteswararao
Author(s): R. C. Moharana, Sk. Soyeb Ali, V. K. Sahu, J. Link, I. Heinmaa, S. Chattopadhyay, S. K. Panda, R. Stern, T. Chakrabarty, and B. Koteswararao Combining geometrical frustration and one-dimensional magnetism boosts quantum fluctuations and leads to unusual states of quantum matter. We report the magnetic behavior of a well-separated S=frac52 sawtooth spin chain compound CaFe₂O(PO₄)₂ (CFPO).… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 115137] Published Wed Mar 18, 2026

item.title: Chemical Doping Engineering of Polarization and Topological Textures in van der Waals Ferroelectric CuInP2S6
item.description:
📚 Wiley: Advanced Science: Table of Contents
👤 Lei Gao, Wenhao Li, Fei Sun, Ziyi Zhou, Zhihao Shen, Jianwei Liang, He Jiang, Weiming Xiong, Weijin Chen, Xiaoyue Zhang, Yi Zhang, Xinzhi Liu, Yue Zheng
Advanced Science, Volume 13, Issue 16, 18 March 2026.

item.title: Field-free perpendicular magnetization switching driven by the anisotropic spin Hall effect in RuO₂(101)
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Zeyuan Zhang, Ziqiang Wang, Jun Cheng, Jiyu Shen, Man Yang, Xi Yang, Yulun Zeng, Mengxue Jiang, Longqian Yu, Junwei Ma, Liang Sun, Gong Chen, Bingfeng Miao, and Haifeng Ding
Author(s): Zeyuan Zhang, Ziqiang Wang, Jun Cheng, Jiyu Shen, Man Yang, Xi Yang, Yulun Zeng, Mengxue Jiang, Longqian Yu, Junwei Ma, Liang Sun, Gong Chen, Bingfeng Miao, and Haifeng Ding Recently, (101)-oriented RuO₂ films have been intensively studied because of their ability to generate a spin current with out-of-plane polarization, enabling field-free switching of perpendicular magnetization. This unconventional spin current has typically been attributed … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 104435] Published Wed Mar 18, 2026

item.title: Frustrated Ising model on the honeycomb lattice: Metastability and universality
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Denis Gessert, Martin Weigel, and Wolfhard Janke
Author(s): Denis Gessert, Martin Weigel, and Wolfhard Janke We study the Ising model with competing ferromagnetic nearest- and antiferromagnetic next-nearest-neighbor interactions of strengths J₁>0 and J₂<0, respectively, on the honeycomb lattice. For J₂>−J₁/4 it has a ferromagnetic ground state, and previous work has shown… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 104436] Published Wed Mar 18, 2026

item.title: Hybrid collective excitations in topological superconductor/ferromagnetic insulator heterostructures
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 T. Karabassov, I. V. Bobkova, A. M. Bobkov, A. S. Vasenko, and A. A. Golubov
Author(s): T. Karabassov, I. V. Bobkova, A. M. Bobkov, A. S. Vasenko, and A. A. Golubov We develop a linear response theory for the dynamical proximity effect in topological superconductor/ferromagnetic insulator (TS/FI) hybrids. Our approach integrates the nonequilibrium quasiclassical Keldysh-Usadel formalism for the TS with the Landau-Lifshitz-Gilbert equation for the FI's magnetiza… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 104514] Published Wed Mar 18, 2026

item.title: Josephson diode effect in unconventional p-wave magnet/superconductor Josephson junctions
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Zhe Yang and Zhi Ping Niu
Author(s): Zhe Yang and Zhi Ping Niu Motivated by recent advancements in the Josephson diode effect and unconventional p-wave magnets (UPMs), we investigate the supercurrent in UPM/superconductor Josephson junctions by the Green's function method. The simultaneous presence of an out-of-plane Zeeman field and Rashba spin-orbit couplin… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 094515] Published Wed Mar 18, 2026

item.title: Magnetic Resonance Imaging Reveals Meningeal Lymphatic Impairment in Lung Adenocarcinoma Brain Metastasis Progression

Advanced Science, Volume 13, Issue 16, 18 March 2026.

item.title: Understanding nematicity and band structure of FeSe by first-principles study and tight-binding model
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Research
👤 Miao-Miao Li, Xiu-Cai Jiang, and Yu-Zhong Zhang
Author(s): Miao-Miao Li, Xiu-Cai Jiang, and Yu-Zhong Zhang We systematically investigate the effects of spin order and orbital polarization on the electronic and magnetic structures of nematic FeSe using the LDA+U method. While our calculations with on-site Coulomb repulsion U∼4.5phantomůle0.28em0exeV can account for both the … [Phys. Rev. Research 8, 013298] Published Wed Mar 18, 2026

item.title: [ASAP] Li–P–S Electrolyte Materials as a Benchmark for Machine-Learned Interatomic Potentials
item.description:
📚 ACS
👤 Natascia L. Fragapane and Volker L. Deringer
Journal of Chemical Theory and Computation DOI: 10.1021/acs.jctc.5c02006
